free_text: Experimental determination of the ion-ion potential in
the N=50 target region: A tool to probe ground-state
nuclear densities
Federal University of Mato Grosso.
Details of
experiments.
Details of
experiments.
The thickness of the carbon,10 mug/cm**2, backed Sr-88,
Zr-90, Zr-92, Mo-92 target were about 40 mug/cm**2,with
a layer of gold, 50 mug/cm**2, were used.
.Deduced optical potentials.
Shell model and double-folding calculations.
A set of nine surface barrier detectors.
The San Paulo 8UD Pelletron accelerator.
- By authors:  due to the high precision required for
the experimental data, the following cares were taken
into account in the data acquisition and reduction-
the use of two monitor detectors to be sure that no
target deterioration occurred during the bombardment.
High-energy resolution to allow a complete separation
between the elastic and inelastic(2+) peaks and
separate the elastic and inelastic peaks from the other
elastic peaks associated with the contaminants in the
targets.
Corrections in the counting rate related to the elastic
and inelastic processes due to the small background
near those peaks.
No information on sources of uncertainty
Last checking has been done.
SD: Small correction in REFERENCE. ERR-T ->
